mcqs.question: What does an object reference variable store?

options.text: The memory address of the object

mcqs.correctAnswer: The memory address of the object
mcqs.explanation: Object reference variables store references (memory addresses) that point to objects in heap memory, not the objects themselves.

mcqs.question: After executing: Object a = new Object(); Object b = a; What is the relationship between a and b?

options.text: They point to the same object

mcqs.correctAnswer: They point to the same object
mcqs.explanation: Assigning one reference to another makes both variables reference the same object in memory (aliasing).

mcqs.question: What is the default value of an instance reference variable?
mcqs.explanation: Instance reference variables are automatically initialized to null by default in Java.

mcqs.question: What is aliasing in Java?

options.text: Two references pointing to the same object

mcqs.correctAnswer: Two references pointing to the same object
mcqs.explanation: Aliasing occurs when two or more reference variables refer to the same object in memory.

mcqs.question: What will be the output? String s1 = new String("Hello"); String s2 = new String("Hello"); System.out.println(s1 == s2);
mcqs.explanation: == compares references, not contents. Since s1 and s2 are different objects, == returns false even though contents are equal.

mcqs.question: Which memory area stores objects in Java?
mcqs.explanation: Objects are stored in heap memory, while primitive variables and reference variables are stored in stack memory.

mcqs.question: What does the 'this' reference refer to?
mcqs.explanation: 'this' is a reference that refers to the current object instance on which the method is being called.

mcqs.question: Which statement about reference assignment is correct?

options.text: It makes both references point to the same object

mcqs.correctAnswer: It makes both references point to the same object
mcqs.explanation: Reference assignment does not create new objects; it makes both variables reference the same object.

mcqs.question: What is printed? Integer a = 100; Integer b = 100; System.out.println(a == b);
mcqs.explanation: Java caches Integer values from -128 to 127. Since both a and b reference the same cached object, == returns true.

mcqs.question: What is shallow copy?

options.text: New object with copied references to nested objects

mcqs.correctAnswer: New object with copied references to nested objects
mcqs.explanation: Shallow copy creates a new object but copies references to nested objects, not the nested objects themselves.

mcqs.question: What is deep copy?

options.text: Completely independent copy including nested objects

mcqs.correctAnswer: Completely independent copy including nested objects
mcqs.explanation: Deep copy creates a fully independent copy of an object including all nested objects.

mcqs.question: When is an object eligible for garbage collection?

options.text: When it goes out of scope
When no references point to it

mcqs.correctAnswer: When no references point to it
mcqs.explanation: An object becomes eligible for garbage collection when there are no more references pointing to it.

mcqs.question: What happens if you call a method on a null reference?
mcqs.explanation: Calling a method on a null reference throws NullPointerException because there's no object to invoke the method on.

mcqs.question: What is the output? StringBuilder sb1 = new StringBuilder("Java"); StringBuilder sb2 = sb1; sb1.append("Script"); System.out.println(sb2);
mcqs.explanation: sb1 and sb2 reference the same object. When sb1 is modified, sb2 sees the same change because they point to the same object.

mcqs.question: What does comparing two references with == check?
mcqs.explanation: The == operator for object references checks if both references point to the same memory location (same object).

mcqs.question: What is reference variable declaration without initialization?
mcqs.explanation: Declaring a reference variable without initialization creates a reference that points to null (no object).

mcqs.question: After: int[] arr = new int[5]; What is arr[0]?
mcqs.explanation: For primitive arrays, elements are initialized to default values (0 for numeric types, false for boolean, '\0' for char).

mcqs.question: What is pass-by-reference in Java for objects?

options.text: Passes a copy of the object

mcqs.explanation: Java passes object references by value (a copy of the reference), meaning the method can modify the object through the reference.

mcqs.question: What is the result of: Object o = null; System.out.println(o);
mcqs.explanation: Printing a null reference prints the string "null". It doesn't throw an exception unless you try to use the null reference.

mcqs.question: Which creates independent object copies?
mcqs.explanation: Deep copy or properly implemented clone() method creates independent object copies, unlike reference assignment or shallow copy.
